answer:
Netlify uses OAuth 2.0 for API authentication [1][2]. For manual requests, you must include a personal access token (PAT) in the Authorization header using the Bearer scheme [1][3].

Example request:

```bash
curl -X GET 'https://api.netlify.com/api/v1/sites' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_NETLIFY_TOKEN' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
```

You can generate a PAT in your Netlify user settings under Applications > Personal access tokens [1][4]. For public integrations, you must use the OAuth 2.0 flow via the authorization endpoint at https://app.netlify.com/authorize [1]. If you reset your password, all existing tokens are invalidated [4].

results.title: Get started with the Netlify API | Netlify Docs
